course_description:
A block placement to develop competency in practical components of speech pathology such as professional development, effective communication skills, assessment, planning and implementation of intervention.

This placement course offers you clinical learning opportunities and experience in speech pathology practice environments. The learning activity for this course is an intensive clinical block placement.ﾠPlease familiarise yourself with the learning objectives for this course.

Clinics provide a rich learning environment in which you demonstrate the development of clinical skills, knowledge and professional attributes in your work with clients with disorders of communication and/or swallowing. You will receive both formative and summative assessment in the course (see COMPASS(R) information on the community Blackboard site). It is expected that you will prepare thoroughly for this clinical work, that you will demonstrate integration of knowledge and practice skills and that the service you provide to clients will be professional, of a high quality and will be informed by principles of evidence-based practice. You are encouraged to keep a learning journal and to reflect on your clinical experiences and the feedback you receive from practice educators and clients.

timetable:
Additional timetable information
Please refer to InPlace for your clinical placement start date.
Your hours in the placement will be determined by the clinical setting and your practice educator. Planning and preparation may be required outside of those working hours.
course_aims: This course focuses on the development of competencies that you require to practise as a speech pathologist in the workplace. It has an emphasis on the development of professional competencies (COMPASS(R) units 1-4) and on SPA PS Domains/Standards 3.2-3.5 and CBOS Units 1-4 (assessment, analysis and interpretation, planning evidence-based speech pathology practices and implementing such practices) andﾠ SPA PS Domains/Standards 2.1-2.7 and CBOS Unitﾠ7 (lifelong learning and reflective practice) of the Competency Based Occupational Standards for Speech Pathologists (CBOS, 2011). You will also have opportunities to develop an understanding of the delivery of speech pathology services in the workplace (Unit 5 of CBOS). You may also have the opportunity to participate in workplace community education activities if these are part of your clinic's service (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.6 andﾠUnit 6 of CBOS). Please familiarise yourself with the CBOS document and the evaluation documents for this course.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate positive professional relationships with clients, colleagues and the wider community, and the ability to adapt communication style for each target audience.
Demonstrate competency in giving clear and concise reports (both written and verbal) that are appropriate to the intended audience.
Demonstrate competency in evaluating relatively straightforward communication disorders, with respect to both formal and informal evaluation procedures.
Demonstrate competency in either describing or diagnosing the client's communication problem, and in using this information to plan and/or deliver a speech pathology management program.
Demonstrate a holistic approach to case management, through knowledge of contributing factors or clinical behaviours which may be affecting communication performance.

Demonstrate competency in all aspects of planning and implementation of therapy relating to the caseload seen at your clinical placement, particularly with respect to decision-making in clinical situations.
Demonstrate an appropriate level of professional, ethical and social behaviour in the clinical situation.

Evaluate your own performance and show a steady and gradual progression in your clinical development as well as understanding the role of this clinical course in your development of independent professional skills.
Demonstrate understanding of the role of other professionals and liaise with such professionals in the holistic management of clients presenting with communication and swallowing disorders.

assessment_summary.title: COMPASS(R) Midway Placement Evaluation
assessment_summary.due_date: 2/03/2026 - 6/03/2026; The midway evaluation will be scheduled at approximately the midway point of placement by your practice educator. Your educator will inform you of the date/time of your meeting and the date/time for which you need to complete your COMPASS(R) self-evaluation.

assessment_summary.title: COMPASS(R) Final Clinical Evaluation
assessment_summary.due_date: 23/03/2026 - 27/03/2026; The final evaluation will be scheduled at the end of your placement by your practice educator. Your educator will inform you of the date/time of your meeting and the date/time for which you need to complete your COMPASS(R) self-evaluation and ePortfolio submissions. Practice educators should complete their final evaluation of students using COMPASS Online within 1 week of placement conclusion.

assessment_details.due_date: 23/03/2026 - 27/03/2026 The final evaluation will be scheduled at the end of your placement by your practice educator. Your educator will inform you of the date/time of your meeting and the date/time for which you need to complete your COMPASS(R) self-evaluation and ePortfolio submissions. assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Work integrated learning, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be assessed at the end of the block placement using COMPASS®2 Online. Students are required to complete self-evaluation prior to their meeting with their practice educator. Practice educators should complete their final assessments for students 1 week after the completion of the placement. At the conclusion of the placement, students are required to: 1) Submit their completed ePortfolio placement reflection; and 2) Submit their ePortfolio Clinical Showcase to their clinical educator. Under no circumstances should identifiable clinical information be uploaded to open or external AI platforms, as it is unethical and irresponsible. Given this assessment is related to a placement with clinical information please see more information on use of AI in student placements under additional assessment information. Practice educators should complete their final assessments for students 1 week after the completion of the placement. At the conclusion of the placement, students are required to: 1) Submit their completed ePortfolio placement reflection; and 2) Submit their ePortfolio Clinical Showcase to their clinical educator. Under no circumstances should identifiable clinical information be uploaded to open or external AI platforms, as it is unethical and irresponsible.
